seoTitle: Parker Range vs Parkeston — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Parker Range and Parkeston. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Parker Range vs Parkeston: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Parker Range and Parkeston.
heading: Parker Range vs Parkeston — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Parker Range, Western Australia and Parkeston, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Parkeston has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Parker Range (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Parker Range or Parkeston safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Parker Range has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Parkeston scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Parker Range scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Parkeston scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Parker Range or Parkeston?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Parkeston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
